data.title: Travis Kelce Wants You to Ride More Roller Coasters — Market Talk
data.description: Travis Kelce is doubling down on his commitment to Six Flags, signing a deal to serve as a brand ambassador for the amusement-park chain. The NFL star will this year partner with Six Flags to help market the company's parks, such as by supplying digital content for social-media platforms. Six Flags will also have the right to utilize Kelce's name, image and likeness across channels including broadcast media, streaming platforms and in-park marketing outlets. Terms of the deal weren't disclosed. It comes after the Kansas City Chiefs tight end last fall became a part owner of Six Flags through his team-up with activist investor Jana Partners. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: ConnectM gains $1M follow-on order from Greentech, cumulative commitments now ~$3.6M
data.description:
ConnectM announced a $1 million purchase order from Greentech Renewables, bringing cumulative commitments for Keen heat pumps to approximately $3.6 million.

Key Highlights:

  • Greentech Renewables Danbury placed an additional $1.0M order for Keen high-efficiency heat pumps and smart controls.
  • The new order raises cumulative commitments to about $3.6M (includes $1.7M initial and $865K follow-on orders).
  • Products are Keen Labs’ smart heat pumps targeting residential and light commercial electrification demand.
  • ConnectM cites Greentech’s contractor network and nationwide footprint as a scaling platform for adoption.

Original SEC Filing: ConnectM Technology Solutions, Inc. [ CNTM ] - 8-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: Daimler Truck shares jump on outlook, orders
data.description:
The German manufacturer of trucks and buses reported a 29% fall in adjusted operating profit to €780m on the back of an 11% decline in revenue to €12bn.

Sales at Trucks North America fell 29%, while revenue at its Mercedes-Benz Trucks unit fell 1%. Group full-year sales for 2025 were down 8% to 422,510 trucks and buses.

However, incoming orders in the final quarter of the year rose 13%, driven by the Mercedes-Benz Trucks unit and a recovery in North America, Daimler Truck said.

The company said revenue this year from its industrial business is expected to range between €42bn - 46bn compared with €45.91bn last year, while adjusted operating profit was forecast in a range of €3.2bn and €3.7bn compared with €3.78bn last year.

It also expects its adjusted return on sales in the industrial business to be between 6% - 8%, compared with 7.9% in 2025.

"The outlook continues to be subject to macroeconomic and geopolitical developments, in particular possible effects from the US trade policy," it said.

It added that the 2026 outlook was subject to macroeconomic and geopolitical developments, especially US tariffs, and excluded the potential impact of the US-Israel war on Iran.

Chief financial officer Eva Scherer said that US tariffs had hit results by around €250m and warned the hit would be significantly higher this year after the introduction of 25% US import tariffs by President Donald Trump starting in November last year.

"For 2026, we are positioned for operational improvement on higher volumes and efficiency gains compensating materially higher tariff effects," Scherer said in a statement.

Reporting by Frank Prenesti for Sharecast.com

data.title: Leonardo Targets Higher Revenue, Earnings as Defense Order Boom Continues — Update
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud and Cristina Gallardo

Italian defense company Leonardo said it is targeting higher revenue and earnings this year and through the end of the decade, as it forecast defense orders to continue to climb amid Europe's push to rearm.

The company said Thursday that it expects revenue of about 21 billion euros ($24.29 billion) and adjusted earnings before interest and taxes of around 2.03 billion euros this year. The figures compare with 19.5 billion euros in revenue and 1.75 billion euros in adjusted EBIT that Leonardo reported for last year.

Orders are expected to rise to about 25 billion euros from 23.8 billion euros in 2025, the company added.

Leonardo also set out targets for the coming years that call for orders of 32 billion euros, revenue of 30 billion euros and adjusted EBIT of 3.59 billion euros by 2030.

"We now have platforms across every domain: land, space, naval and air, and we have invested heavily in digital technologies, AI and cybersecurity," Chief Executive Officer Roberto Cingolani said.

Shares in Leonardo gained 5.4% in early afternoon trade in Europe, to 63.64 euros.

Leonardo's Michelangelo Dome, its new air-defense technology aimed at helping Europe to create dome-style shields against missile and drone attacks, should generate 21 billion euros over the next decade, the company said. This includes 6 billion euros between 2026 and 2030, and a further 15 billion euros between 2031 and 2035, it added.

The North Atlantic Treaty Organization will start trials for the Michelangelo Dome in 2027, while Ukraine could receive the first component of the system by the end of this year, Leonardo said.

The Michelangelo Dome is designed to boost the protection of critical infrastructure, urban areas and key national assets.

The company said it would invest in its own operations to support growth and strengthen its core business while looking at external opportunities within its strategic growth areas and boosting returns to shareholders.

It declared a dividend of 0.63 euros a share, up 21% on year.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com and Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: Best Buy Could See Challenges from Memory Shortage — Market Talk
data.description: Best Buy is preparing for potential challenges from a shortage of memory, as data centers gobble up more chips. Chief Executive Corie Barry says at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference the company could see unit sales pressure in its computing products due to higher memory costs passing through to higher prices. "I think we're just contemplating, could there be a situation where there are just some constraints, you just actually can't get some of the product," Barry says. "All of that memory is being shifted to commercial uses in a way from some consumer products, which is creating the shortage overall." (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Karman Taps Former L3Harris Executive as Next CEO
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Karman Holdings tapped a former L3Harris Technologies executive to serve as its next chief executive.

The space and defense company said Thursday that Jon Rambeau will assume its top role on March 23, as CEO Tony Koblinski is set to retire following a 40-year career.

Rambeau has held C-suite and senior executive roles with two of the world's top defense contractors, Karman said. He most recently helmed L3Harris Technologies' communications and spectrum dominance unit, and prior to that, oversaw its integrated mission systems business.

Prior, Rambeau spent more than 25 years at Lockheed Martin, where he held senior executive roles across a broad spectrum of defense capabilities, Karman said.

He will succeed Koblinski, who has led Karman since its founding in 2020.

While serving as CEO, Koblinski oversaw the company's IPO and multiple acquisitions, as well as helped solidify its position as a trusted partner by more than 80 customers across both space and defense markets, Chairman David Stinnett said.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Helium prices soar as Qatar LNG halt exposes fragile supply chain
data.description:
By Arunima Kumar, Sumit Saha and Toby Sterling

Disruptions to Qatar's natural gas processing from the Iran war have driven helium prices sharply higher, exposing the fragility of a small but critical market that supports industries from semiconductors to medical imaging.

Helium spot prices have doubled since the Middle East crisis began, according to Phil Kornbluth, president of Kornbluth Helium Consulting, as buyers scramble to secure supply.

State energy giant QatarEnergy, the world's second-largest LNG exporter, announced a production halt at its 77 million tons per annum (mtpa) facility last week and declared force majeure on LNG shipments, amid ​the conflict.

Because helium is extracted as a byproduct of natural gas processing, any disruption to LNG output directly cuts helium supply.

Qatari Energy Minister Saad al-Kaabi told the Financial Times last week that it would take "weeks to months" for deliveries to return to normal even if the conflict ended immediately.

Qatar is a pivotal supplier rather than a marginal one.

Data from the U.S. Geological Survey shows the country produced about 63 million cubic meters of helium in 2025, out of roughly 190 million cubic meters globally, accounting for close to one-third of world supply.

"If those conditions (supply disruption) persist, the market is effectively missing about 5.2 million cubic meters of helium per month," said Aleksandr Romanenko, CEO of market research firm IndexBox.

The disruption is reverberating through a market with little spare production capacity and limited storage, leaving buyers with few short-term alternatives.

Japan's top helium supplier Iwatani said it had so far maintained stable supply to customers including semiconductor manufacturers, partly because it also sources helium from the United States and maintains stockpiles in both Japan and the U.S.

A MARKET BUILT ON CONTRACTS, NOT TRANSPARENCY

Helium markets operate very differently from most commodities.

Most supply is sold through long-term contracts rather than a transparent spot market, meaning price signals often emerge slowly even as supply tightens.

That opacity makes price discovery difficult, but signs of tightening supply have already begun to emerge.

"Early indications show about 50% spot price increases already," said Anish Kapadia, CEO of market research firm AKAP Energy.

"In a sustained disruption, prices could rise sharply and potentially retest past shortage peaks of more than $2,000 per thousand cubic feet."

Romanenko said a 30-day disruption could lift delivered helium prices by 10% to 20%, while a 60-to-90-day outage could push prices higher by 25% to 50%, particularly for buyers without long-term supply contracts.

Helium's physical properties add another constraint. The gas is typically shipped in liquid form and gradually evaporates during transport.

"It's a commodity, but it also has a shelf life," said Chris Bakker, CEO of helium developer Avanti .

"So when you liquefy it, and that's how they tend to ship it worldwide, you've got notionally 45 days to get it to the end-user."

CRITICAL INDUSTRIES FIRST IN LINE

If supply tightens further, suppliers typically prioritise key sectors when allocating volumes during force majeure events.

Kornbluth said industries such as medical MRI systems and rocket ships would probably get 100% of their needs, while semiconductor manufacturers might receive 95%.

Lower-priority uses, including welding, diving equipment and party balloons, would likely face deeper cuts.

Last week, South Korea's ruling party lawmaker Kim Young-bae warned the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could disrupt supplies of key semiconductor manufacturing materials, giving helium as one example.

The hierarchy reflects helium's role in technologies where substitutes are limited.

Industrial gas companies sourcing helium from Qatar, including Air Liquide, Linde and Air Products and Chemicals , are among those most exposed to the supply shock, said Kapadia.

Air Products said it was taking steps to ensure continuity of supply but did not give further details.

Linde declined to comment, while Air Liquide said it relied on multiple sources in different continents and on its storage cavern in Europe.

Iwatani Japan is also exposed, Kornbluth and Bakker said.

Producers outside the region could benefit if disruptions persist.

Exxon Mobil is the largest helium producer outside Qatar, while Canada-based North American Helium and smaller developers such as Helix Exploration and Blue Star Helium could see stronger demand, Kapadia added.

data.title: WesBanco, Inc. Advances Organic Growth Strategy with Commercial Banking Expansion in South Florida
data.description:
Seasoned banking team with deep market expertise joins to accelerate growth

WHEELING, W.Va., March 12, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — WesBanco, Inc. , a diversified, multi-state bank holding company, today announced the expansion of its commercial banking business across key high-growth South Florida markets, initially including Palm Beach and Broward counties, with the hiring of a seasoned team of commercial banking leaders and support staff. This expansion further advances WesBanco's organic growth strategy and commitment to serving clients in attractive, relationship-driven markets and is additive to its longer-term financial outlook.

"These South Florida markets are significant growth centers, and ones I have come to know well during my banking career," said Jeff Jackson, President and Chief Executive Officer, WesBanco. "We are excited to enter these markets with this seasoned team of bankers, whose customer focus and values are well-aligned with our relationship-led approach and strong credit standards. Having worked with these bankers in prior roles, I have seen firsthand their commitment to clients and have great respect for their market reputation. I am confident they will be an exceptional fit as we expand into South Florida."

The new team brings deep relationship management experience and regional market expertise to support middle-market and commercial clients with customized financial solutions, insight, and exceptional service. Leading the South Florida team, Eddy Rodriguez joins WesBanco as Executive Vice President and Regional President. Rodriguez brings nearly 30 years of commercial banking experience with leading financial institutions to WesBanco.

Leading the markets are:

  • Steven Goldstein, Market President and Senior Commercial Banker, focused on Palm Beach County and with more than three decades of experience in commercial financial services.
  • Tony Mandravellos, Market President and Senior Commercial Banker, focused on Broward County and with nearly 30 years of experience in commercial financial services.

The new South Florida team also includes specialists in commercial lending, credit underwriting and client relationship support — bringing together decades of collective expertise to ensure comprehensive, high-touch service as WesBanco builds its presence in the region.

WesBanco's expansion in South Florida complements its recent growth through strategic acquisitions and the opening of loan production offices. Over the past several years, the Company has expanded its scale and reach in Indiana, Michigan, and Ohio through its acquisition of Premier Financial Corp., and in Northern Virginia and Nashville, Chattanooga, and Knoxville, Tennessee through targeted investments in new loan production offices.

"Our South Florida expansion is a thoughtful extension of our long-stated southeastern expansion strategy," said Jay Zatta, Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Banking Officer, WesBanco. "Many of our in-footprint clients already have meaningful ties to the region, from second homes to operating interests. Establishing a regional presence with this proven team allows us to support those relationships more directly while advancing our growth strategy through new relationships."

As the regional business develops, WesBanco expects to evaluate additional services and solutions, including retail financial centers, treasury, wealth management, and mortgage offerings, to deliver even greater value to clients.

About WesBanco, Inc.

With over 150 years as a community-focused, regional financial services partner, WesBanco Inc. and its subsidiaries build lasting prosperity through relationships and solutions that empower our customers for success in their financial journeys. Customers across our ten-state footprint choose WesBanco for the comprehensive range and personalized delivery of our retail and commercial banking solutions, as well as trust, brokerage, wealth management and insurance services, all designed to advance their financial goals. Through the strength of our teams, we leverage large bank capabilities and local focus to help make every community we serve a better place for people and businesses to thrive. Headquartered in Wheeling, West Virginia, WesBanco has $27.7 billion in total assets, with our Trust and Investment Services holding $7.9 billion of assets under management and securities account values (including annuities) of $2.5 billion through our broker/dealer, as of December 31, 2025. Learn more at www.wesbanco.com and follow @WesBanco on Facebook, LinkedIn and Instagram.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/wesbanco-inc-advances-organic-growth-strategy-with-commercial-banking-expansion-in-south-florida-302711584.html

SOURCE WesBanco, Inc.

data.title: Nvidia unveils Nemotron 3 Super AI model
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA, Financials) has introduced a new artificial intelligence model called Nemotron 3 Super, designed to support complex agentic AI systems that can perform multi-step tasks autonomously.

The company said the model combines advanced reasoning capabilities with a hybrid mixture-of-experts architecture, allowing it to run AI workloads more efficiently while maintaining high accuracy. According to Nvidia, Nemotron 3 Super can deliver up to five times higher throughput and twice the accuracy compared with its earlier Nemotron Super model.

The system includes 120 billion parameters, though only about 12 billion are active during inference. This design allows the model to reduce computational costs while still maintaining strong performance in demanding AI applications.

Several technology and enterprise software companies are already exploring the new model. Palantir, Cadence Design Systems and Dell Technologies are working to deploy and customize the system for tasks such as cybersecurity automation, semiconductor design and enterprise AI workflows. Hewlett Packard Enterprise is also integrating the technology into its enterprise AI infrastructure tools.

Nvidia said the model features a one-million-token context window, allowing AI agents to retain large amounts of information while executing complex tasks.

data.title: HMRC explores crypto forensic tools for tax probes as former OECD advisor flags UK’s growing compliance push
data.description:
His Majesty's Revenue and Customs is exploring the procurement of crypto forensic software and investigative support services as part of efforts to combat money laundering and tax fraud involving digital assets.

The market engagement notice, published on the UK government's tender platform this week, outlines a potential procurement for licenses to blockchain tracing software, forensic investigative support, and tools that allow investigators to query blockchain data at scale.

The initiative is intended to support HMRC's Fraud Investigation Service and Risk and Intelligence Service, which handle serious fraud investigations and assess risks to the UK tax system.

HMRC said the tools under consideration would enable capabilities such as automated triage of cases, deep forensic analytics, cross-chain transaction tracing, attribution and risk scoring, and advanced data visualization. The department also highlighted the need for secure and compliant operations, along with training and administrative oversight for investigators.

Potential contracts worth more than $4.6 million

The tax authority is considering procuring two contracts through a single approach, with a combined estimated value of £3.42 million ($4.6 million), excluding value-added tax, over three years.

The contracts are expected to run from April 1, 2027, to March 31, 2029, with a possible extension through March 31, 2030. One contract would provide 55 licenses and 550 hours of investigative support annually, while the second would include 20 licenses and 200 hours of support, alongside training programs for investigators.

The notice is part of a preliminary process designed to gather insights from potential suppliers before any formal tender is issued. HMRC said participation in the exercise would not influence future procurement decisions and noted that the final scope and timeline could change.

Commenting on the development, Colby Mangels, global head of government solutions at Taxbit and a former OECD advisor, said the initiative reflects a broader shift among governments toward building compliance infrastructure for digital assets.

"HMRC's latest engagement with the crypto industry reflects a broader shift that we're seeing globally: governments recognize that digital assets are becoming a significant part of the financial landscape, which requires modern compliance infrastructure," Mangels told The Block. "What's significant about this engagement is the level of sophistication HMRC is seeking."

He added that investigations increasingly require cross-chain tracing, forensic analytics, and real-time blockchain data access as authorities seek to move "as quickly and intelligently as the technology they're policing."

Mangels said the investment aligns with the UK government's ambition to become a global crypto hub, noting the country recently became one of the first jurisdictions to implement the OECD's Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework for overseas transaction data sharing.

"All of these steps are a clear signal that the UK intends to lead by example for crypto compliance, enforcement, and transparency," he said.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2026 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: ConnectM Tech Solutions Files 8K - Regulation FD >CNTM
data.description:
ConnectM Technology Solutions Inc. (CNTM) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 12, 2026.

On March 12, 2026, ConnectM Technology Solutions, Inc., (the "Company") announced by press release that, Greentech Renewables has placed an additional $1 million purchase order for Keen high-efficiency heat pumps and related smart controls developed by Keen Labs, ConnectM's AI and technology subsidiary. The order, placed through Greentech Renewables Danbury, increases the parties' cumulative commitments to approximately $3.6 million, inclusive of the $1.7 million initial purchase order announced on November 10, 2025 and the $865,000 follow-on order announced on January 27, 2026.

The press release is furnished herewith as Exhibit 99.1 and is incorporated by reference herein. The information contained in the press release is being furnished and shall not be deemed filed for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), or otherwise subject to the liability of that Section, and shall not be incorporated by reference into any registration statement or other document filed under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Exchange Act, except as shall be expressly set forth by specific reference in such filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895249/000110465926026796/tm267206d4_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895249/000110465926026796/0001104659-26-026796-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Microsoft May Tap Massive AI Campus
data.description:
Microsoft may soon join the growing list of tech giants circling the massive Stargate data center project in Abilene, Texas, as demand for AI computing power keeps climbing.

According to a report from The Information, Microsoft is in advanced talks to lease hundreds of megawatts of capacity from the unfinished site. The interest comes after Meta Platforms was also reported to be looking at space there following a decision by Oracle and OpenAI not to expand the project from 1.2 GW to the originally planned 2 GW.

The Abilene location is one of the centerpiece sites of the Stargate Project, a large scale AI infrastructure initiative. The roughly 1,000 acre campus is being developed by Crusoe, a company that focuses on building data centers powered by renewable energy and stranded natural gas.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Sued by Former Employees Over Monte Paschi Case
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

Deutsche Bank said four former employees sued it seeking damages over a longrunning case that stemmed from transactions between German bank and Italy's Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena in 2008.

The four former employees filed claims in the English courts in September alleging Italian court proceedings over the transactions hurt their careers, and seeking more than 600 million pounds ($804.7 million) in damages, Deutsche Bank said in its annual report published Thursday. The lender said it considers the claims to be without merit and called the alleged losses claimed unrealistic.

The suits followed a previous claim in Germany in 2024 by another former employee who sought 152 million euros ($175.8 million) in connection to the case, according to the report.

Deutsche Bank said a sixth former employee filed a claim in the U.K., but the parties resolved all of the employee's claims and allegations on a confidential basis.

The six former employees were convicted in 2019 by the Court of First Instance of Milan for their role in transactions Deutsche Bank had entered into with Monte Paschi transactions, but higher courts later acquitted the defendants.

Deutsche Bank said it hasn't disclosed whether it has established a provision or contingent liability in relation to the litigations because such disclosure could affect their outcome.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Saga Communications Q4 revenue falls 9.3% on ad market weakness
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. radio broadcaster's Q4 revenue fell 9.3% yr/yr as overall ad market softened

  • Company posted Q4 net loss after $20.4 mln impairment charge on goodwill and licenses

  • Saga completed sale of telecom towers, recognizing $11.6 mln gain

Outlook

  • Saga expects capital expenditures of $3.5 mln to $4.5 mln for 2026

  • Company intends to pay regular quarterly cash dividends in the future

  • Saga Board will continue to consider special dividends and stock buybacks

Result Drivers

  • RADIO ADVERTISING WEAKNESS - Co said lower than expected revenue growth in radio advertising drove a $20.4 mln impairment charge and weighed on Q4 results

  • DIGITAL REVENUE GROWTH - Digital revenue increased 25.8% yr/yr to $4.3 mln in Q4, partially offsetting declines in other segments

  • LOW POLITICAL ADVERTISING - Gross political revenue fell sharply in Q4, as is typical in non-election years, further reducing overall revenue

Company press release:

Analyst Coverage

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "buy"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the broadcasting peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Saga Communications Inc is $18.00, about 63.6% above its March 11 closing price of $11.00

  • The stock recently traded at 23 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 25 three months ago

data.title: Unit Corporation Q4 net income jumps on asset sale
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. oil and gas producer's Q4 revenue was flat yr/yr, net income jumped on asset sale

  • Company sold contract drilling unit, results from that unit shown as discontinued operations

  • Unit declared Q1 2026 quarterly dividend and repurchased shares in 2025

Result Drivers

  • OIL AND NGL PRICES - Lower average realized prices for oil and NGLs weighed on segment revenue

  • NATURAL GAS PRICES - Higher average realized natural gas prices supported segment revenue

  • OIL PRODUCTION - Increased oil production partially offset lower prices

Company press release:
